text.content:
Caelith presents these five choices on the morning of Day 2, immediately after the debrief and Aldea's reply. He does not direct the party. He lays out the choices, describes the consequences of each, and abides by the party's decision. Killing Pell is treated as a serious moral and political failure unless extreme circumstances justify it (see below).

Choice 1 — Expose Pell publicly

The party informs the Audit Hall (or the Council, or both) that Pell has been leaking. Pell is dismissed within a day; the Council formally censures him; he loses his stipend, his pension prospects, and his position. Sera Pell is fully safe — the conspiracy has no further reason to threaten her, since Pell can no longer access Caelith's office.

  • Pros: Moral clarity. Sera is permanently safe. The leak is closed cleanly.
  • Cons: The conspiracy switches to a new courier and (within months) recruits a new clerk in a different office. The party loses tactical use of the leak. Caelith is publicly seen to have employed a leak for three weeks without acting — politically harmful to him, regardless of his reasons.
  • Caelith's view: "If you choose this, I will not argue. I will help Sera find employment outside the city, since she will not want to remain in a shop her brother brought down."

Choice 2 — Turn Pell

The party confronts Pell privately, offers Sera physical protection, and recruits Pell as a double agent. Pell will agree if the party convinces him Sera is genuinely safe. He becomes a knowing source — Caelith's office continues to forward summaries, but the summaries are now drafted with the party's strategic input.

  • Pros: Real intelligence flow on the courier chain. Operational control over what the conspiracy reads. Sera lives.
  • Cons: Sera must be visibly protected for the rest of Phase 5 — at minimum, two PCs within earshot of her shop at all times she is on the street, plus a credible second exit through the alley. The handler may attempt a snap-extraction of Sera if the conspiracy suspects Pell has flipped (Pressure Clock segment 4 risk). Pell himself may flip back if his nerve breaks; let players decide whether to trust him.
  • Caelith's view: "Turning him is the cleanest active use of the leak. It requires you to actually protect Sera — not promise to. If you cannot commit to that, choose Choice 3 instead."

Choice 3 — Feed Pell false information

The party leaves Pell in place and feeds him a chosen lie from the False-Packet Menu (next page). Pell forwards the lie to the courier; the conspiracy reacts; the party watches. Pell does not know he is being used.

  • Pros: Tactical control over the conspiracy's next move. The leak channel stays open for further use. Sera is at no greater risk than baseline.
  • Cons: Pell remains compromised — Caelith's office stays touched by the enemy by design. The party is morally implicated in continuing to use a man who is being coerced; some players will find this uncomfortable, which is the point.
  • Caelith's view: "This is the choice I am most likely to make. I am telling you that because I want you to be aware of what I am willing to do, and to choose for yourselves."

Choice 4 — Shadow Pell's courier chain

The party does not approach Pell at all. Instead they tail his courier from the dead-drop forward, identifying the next layer up — the handler, Vesten Quill. See The Courier Chain page.

  • Pros: Pell never knows he was watched, so the channel is not at risk of being burned by Pell himself. Identifies Quill, which opens the optional combat encounter (and the receipt handout). Sera is at baseline risk only.
  • Cons: Counter-surveillance risk — Quill is paranoid and may detect a tail. If detected, the courier chain shifts and the party loses the trail. Requires successful Stealth/Investigation work over multiple days.
  • Caelith's view: "If you can do this without being seen, this is the best of the four. If you cannot, fall back to Choice 3."

Choice 5 — Remove Pell from Caelith's office

The party asks Caelith to reassign Pell quietly. Pell is moved to a less-sensitive Audit Hall posting (e.g., the records archive); his access to Caelith's correspondence ends. The handler will read the reassignment as Pell's own choice, not the party's.

  • Pros: The leak ends without a public scandal.
  • Cons: Sera remains exposed. The handler will assume Pell flinched and may move against Sera as a punishment / warning to other recruits. Caelith's office goes blind — the channel closes, the party gets no further use of it. Worst political outcome unless paired with extraction of Sera (which the party must arrange themselves; Caelith no longer has Audit Hall resources to do it for them).
  • Caelith's view: "I will do this if you ask. I will tell you it is the worst of the five unless you have a plan for Sera. Do you have a plan for Sera?"

The unmentioned sixth choice — kill Pell

The party may consider killing Pell. Caelith will not propose it. Pell himself will not provoke it.

Killing Pell is treated as a serious moral and political failure unless extreme circumstances justify it. Extreme circumstances include: Pell himself attacking the party (he will not); Pell caught actively passing live operational intelligence about a witness's location and refusing to stop (possible but unlikely); or Pell being the only viable witness to a crime that will otherwise go unpunished (not the case in Phase 5, since Caelith and the party are themselves witnesses).

If the party kills Pell unjustifiably:

  • Caelith will refuse to work with the party on Phase 5's other vectors. Letter 7 is not delivered. The party leaves Loftwick without Caelith's continuing support.
  • Pressure Clock advances by 2 segments immediately.
  • Sera Pell becomes the GM's responsibility. She will not survive Phase 5 unless the party extracts her without Caelith's help. Her death should land at the table; do not soften it.
  • Trina Alvere will reduce her cooperation for the rest of the campaign. She does not condemn the party; she merely declines to extend further trust.

This choice exists in the system because some parties will choose it, and the system should answer them. It is not endorsed.

If the party chooses to feed Pell false information (Choice 3), they pick from this menu. Caelith provides the menu and writes the actual packet contents — the wording of each lie is a Caelith specialty. The conspiracy reacts within 3–7 days. Each lie creates a different opportunity. The handout version (Handout 3) presents these as a choice card the player picks from physically. Multiple lies may be fed in series if the party wishes — but each repeats the Pressure Clock advancement risk, and the conspiracy will become suspicious of multiple unrelated packets in quick succession.

Lie 1 — "Haskur Vandrell is believed to be in Rel Astra."

The packet: A short summary in Caelith's hand reporting that "a credible source from the Domain has placed Haskur Vandrell in the Foreign Quarter of Rel Astra; investigation of the Aerdi commercial circles is recommended."

Conspiracy reaction (3–4 days): The conspiracy redirects observation eastward. Aerdi commercial-circle watchers shift toward Rel Astra; the Greyhawk City node is left lightly observed for 6–9 days.

Operational consequence: Greyhawk City pursuit (Vector A) becomes substantially easier — the Brass Crow's countersurveillance relaxes; Iren Velash (the manuscript dealer) becomes safe to approach openly; the courier archive becomes accessible without forced entry.

Best paired with: Vector A (Greyhawk City).

Lie 2 — "The party is pursuing the wood correspondent."

The packet: A short summary in Caelith's hand reporting that "the party intends to travel to a woodland correspondent of Trina Alvere's, address withheld, in pursuit of the marginalia analysis."

Conspiracy reaction (4–5 days): The conspiracy attempts to identify and intercept the correspondent. Aldea is endangered — her cottage is approached by a small reconnaissance party (two riders, one observer; the riders do not enter the valley but they are seen near it). Pressure Clock segment 4 fires hard — Aldea leaves a note for the party that she is "going to her sister's for a season" and disappears for the rest of Phase 5.

Operational consequence: The party gains evidence that the conspiracy has surface presence in the Stark Mounds region (the riders can be tracked back to a small inn at the foothills road). Buys time on Vectors A and C — the conspiracy is distracted. Burns Vector B for this phase.

Best paired with: Vector C (Counter-Leak with bait), if the party intends to use Aldea's danger as leverage to expose conspiracy assets near Loftwick. Do not use this lie if Vector B is the party's intended primary path.

Lie 3 — "The party has no usable evidence from Sereth."

The packet: A short summary in Caelith's hand reporting that "the party's recovered Rel Astra evidence is unfit for court use; the matter is presumed closed at the Loftwick end."

Conspiracy reaction (5–7 days): The conspiracy de-prioritizes containment of the party. Aerdi commercial-court witnesses become more accessible (Path A) or Sereth's retirement estate relaxes its watch (Path B). The conspiracy may also decide that Caelith is no longer worth watching closely, which marginally relaxes the Loftwick observation pressure.

Operational consequence: Vector D (Rel Astra Legal Continuation) becomes much easier — the firm is less defended. Vectors A and C also benefit from reduced observation. Vector B is unaffected.

Best paired with: Vector D (Rel Astra Legal Continuation, Path A only).

Lie 4 — "The party intends to expose Lord Galenix Naelax."

The packet: A short summary in Caelith's hand reporting that "the party intends to publicize Lord Galenix Naelax's private debts to Cindren & Vhal as a political move against the Naelax house."

Conspiracy reaction (3–5 days): The conspiracy attempts to extract Galenix from the Naelax embassy or shore up his position. The Naelax embassy in Rel Astra becomes briefly active — observable but not directly accessible from Loftwick.

Operational consequence: Niche. Useful only if the party is in regular Aerdi correspondence. If the party briefed Galenix in Phase 4, this lie is also a betrayal of that briefing — Caelith will explicitly warn the party of this before sending the packet.

Best paired with: tables that briefed Galenix in Phase 4 and now wish to use his position as a misdirection. Use with care.

Lie 5 — "The party is returning to Hardby."

The packet: A short summary in Caelith's hand reporting that "the party is preparing a return trip to Hardby for additional witness work; departure expected within seven days."

Conspiracy reaction (4–6 days): The conspiracy redirects watchers westward to Hardby. Loftwick observation thins for 4–6 days, opening a window for the courier-chain investigation in Loftwick itself. Hardby observation tightens, but the party is not actually there, so the conspiracy's effort is wasted.

Operational consequence: Vector C (Counter-Leak) gains a clear operational window — the courier-chain investigation in Loftwick can be conducted with reduced counter-surveillance during the 4–6 day Hardby-redirection window. Vesten Quill himself does not relax his guard (he is local), but the watchers above him do.

Best paired with: Vector C (Counter-Leak) as the primary lie of the phase.

pages.name: The Courier Chain — Tracing Up From Pell

text.content:
A short investigative sequence, no required combat. Five locations, in approximate sequence. Best run as a Stealth/Investigation/Persuasion crawl over three to five in-game days, with one or more PCs assigned to each location and the others on Sera-protection duty (if Pell has been turned).

Location 1 — The civic copy room

Where: Behind a small Audit Hall annex, two streets from the main Audit Hall building. A ledger-binder rents the front of the building during day hours; the copy room is in the rear and is shared by three Audit Hall departments.

What's here: The dead-drop brick. Third from the bottom, fourth from the left, in a wall that has not been recoursed in fifteen years. The brick is hollow on the back side; a small slip of folded paper fits inside.

Checks:

  • Investigation DC 13 to find the brick on a first sweep (with knowledge of the drop location, this is automatic).
  • Investigation DC 16 to determine when it was last opened (recent fingerprints, faint dust pattern).
  • Stealth DC 14 to enter and leave the copy room unobserved during day hours; DC 11 at night.

What it yields: Confirmation of the dead-drop. The next packet placement schedule (Pell's standard pickup is on the morning following any cold-stove rag signal). A faint scent of pipe-smoke unique to the courier (Investigation DC 15 to associate it later).

Location 2 — The cooper's lane

Where: A narrow service alley behind a journeyman cooper's workshop on the lane that runs between the copy room and the Civic Way. The cooper himself is honest and does not know he is being used as a transit point.

What's here: The courier crosses this alley twice a week — once on pickup days from the dead-drop toward Quill Street, once on Wednesdays from somewhere uphill toward an unknown destination.

Checks:

  • Stealth DC 13 to observe the alley unobserved (multiple vantage points: the cooper's loft, the wall above the lane, a small gap in the rear of a baker's shop).
  • Investigation DC 14 to identify the courier from a description (mid-thirties, brown beard, plain dark cloak, slight forward lean, the pipe-smoke from Location 1).
  • Perception DC 15 to notice that the courier checks the alley for observers before crossing (he is professional, not paranoid).

What it yields: The courier's name (overheard by a baker's apprentice who knows him only as Trell) and his approximate destination (uphill, toward Quill Street).

Location 3 — The civic courier desk

Where: On the Civic Way, a private message service used by a handful of Audit Hall and Council functionaries. Run by an honest woman named Pell Garven (no relation to Ostren — Pell is a common Loftwick surname).

What's here: Trell collects routine messages here for several legitimate clients, including two Audit Hall departments. He uses the desk as cover for his courier-chain pickup of any packet from Location 2.

Checks:

  • Persuasion DC 15 to get Pell Garven (the desk clerk) to confirm Trell's regular pickup pattern. (She is uncomfortable discussing her clients but will cooperate if presented with credible evidence of wrongdoing — e.g., the Aldea reply or the broadside attack.)
  • Insight DC 13 to confirm Pell Garven herself is honest (she is) and not knowingly part of the chain (she is not).

What it yields: Trell's pickup pattern (Tuesday and Friday mornings, between the seventh and eighth bells), his client list (eight clients of which six are legitimate), and the address he uses for his "personal" pickup (a rented room above a printer's shop on Quill Street).

Location 4 — The rented room above the printer's shop

Where: Quill Street, three doors east of the small bridge that crosses the Audit Hall stream. The printer is a quiet old man named Ven Sallis; the upstairs is rented in the name of "V. Quill". The printer himself believes V. Quill is a quiet northern lawyer who keeps unusual hours.

What's here: A transit point. The room is sparsely furnished: a desk, a chair, a small lockable cabinet, a brazier, a narrow cot. Trell delivers packets here; Vesten Quill collects them at his discretion. The room contains no living occupant most of the time. Quill is here perhaps three to five evenings a week.

Checks:

  • Stealth DC 14 to enter the printer's shop after hours (rear alley door; Ven Sallis sleeps in a back room and does not lock the alley door because he doesn't think he needs to).
  • Investigation DC 15 to find anything actionable in the room (the cabinet contains a few coded notes that Caelith can decipher in 1d4 days — these reference a Hardby-aligned letter agency operating out of a Greyhawk City address).
  • Sleight of Hand DC 13 to pick the cabinet lock without forcing it.

What it yields: The Hardby-aligned letter agency reference (the next layer up from Quill); a reading on Quill's evening pattern (he comes between the eleventh and the first bells); enough material for the party to plan an in-room confrontation.

Location 5 — The dead drop near the Hall of Bonds

Where: A sealed lead pipe behind a buttress on the south wall of the Hall of Bonds, two streets east of Quill Street. The pipe is mortared into the wall; only the cap is removable, and the cap is disguised as part of the buttress's drainage.

What's here: The drop where Trell deposits packets for Quill to retrieve. Trell and Quill never meet directly; the pipe is the interface.

Checks:

  • Investigation DC 16 to find the cap among the buttress's other ornamental elements.
  • Sleight of Hand DC 14 to open and close the cap without leaving evidence of tampering.
  • Stealth DC 12 to access the pipe unobserved (the Hall of Bonds has a night-watch but it is sparse).

What it yields: Handout 6 — the compromised courier receipt. A small folded note with a date, a count of pages, and a single initial — "V." — confirming the receiving end is in Quill's network. This is the documentary evidence the party can present to Mairra Voss to support a renewed Council vote against the Closure motion.

The optional combat encounter

If the party corners Vesten Quill at his Quill Street room, the only combat encounter in Phase 5 fires.

Setup: Quill enters the room between the eleventh and first bells. He is alone for the first round if he was not warned; he carries a single Thug bodyguard if he has been warned (e.g., if the party tipped him by tailing too closely). One additional Thug arrives from a neighboring rooftop if the fight extends past round 3 (Quill keeps a whistle and uses it on round 1 if he can).

Combatants:

  • Vesten Quill — CR 2 Bandit Captain stat block, see actor-vesten-quill.json. AC 15 (studded leather), HP 65, Multiattack (scimitar + dagger or two scimitar), Parry reaction, hand crossbow.
  • Thug 1 — CR 1/2 SRD Thug. AC 11, HP 32, mace + heavy crossbow.
  • Thug 2 (round 3+) — CR 1/2 SRD Thug, identical.

Quill's behavior: Will surrender if reduced to half HP (33). Will name the next layer up — a small Hardby-aligned letter agency operating out of a Greyhawk City address — but no farther. Will not name the handler above him (he does not know). Will accept Yeomanry custody if the party offers it; will refuse to be killed in custody (he will request the Council).

If Marshal Thale's whistle response is invoked: Three militia arrive within three minutes of the whistle. They subdue rather than kill. They take prisoners back to the city barracks. The party is required to attend a brief debrief at the barracks the following morning — Marshal Thale's report.

This is the only combat encounter in Phase 5. Tune by ±1 CR for your party's level.
